Ad Text:
Help shape what’s next in data, AI, and advisory within Altis Consulting.   We’re looking for a Principal Consultant to join the team and play a key role in building our Victorian success.  

This is more than a delivery role. We’re looking for someone who can bring strong technical capability, commercial thinking, and a modern advisory mindset to help clients solve real business problems with data. 

Join a growing team that values curiosity, collaboration, and practical innovation. You will be someone who is excited by evolving technology, comfortable navigating ambiguity, and motivated by creating solutions that are not only clever, but sustainable, governed, and valuable in the real world. 

In this role 

You will work closely with clients to understand their challenges, shape solutions, and deliver high-impact outcomes across modern data platforms and AI-enabled environments. 

Your role will include: 

Lead the design and delivery of modern data and analytics solutions 
Partner with clients to translate business problems into practical, scalable strategies 
Provide trusted advisory support, recommendations, and technical leadership 
Work across platforms such as Snowflake, Databricks, and Microsoft Fabric 
Support the development of governed, sustainable, and future-ready data solutions 
Mentor other team members, share expertise, and help foster a high-performing, collaborative team environment  
Engage confidently with stakeholders across both technical and non-technical backgrounds  

What you will bring 

We’re looking for someone who combines strong foundational skills with a modern mindset. 

Lets talk experience 

You will bring experience in consulting, advisory, or client-facing delivery roles within data and analytics (No specific industry) 
Strong capability across modern data platforms, particularly Snowflake, Databricks, and/or Fabric 
Apply strong data engineering and data modelling capability to build scalable, sustainable, and well-governed solutions that turn complex data into practical business value 
Exposure to AI- solutions, or a clear understanding of how AI can be applied responsibly in enterprise environments, is a bonus.  
The ability to solve real-world business challenges, not just build technical outputs 
Confidence in making recommendations, influencing stakeholders, and leading conversations with clients 
A strong understanding of how to deliver solutions that are scalable, practical, and well governed

Added Bonus 

Previous exposure to modern AI-assisted delivery tools (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Cursor, Snowflake Cortex Code or similar) and the judgment to use them responsibly to accelerate delivery while maintaining quality. 

The mindset that matters 

Technical capability is important, but it’s only part of the picture. 

We’re especially interested in people who demonstrate: 

A growth mindset 
Curiosity and a genuine interest in new and emerging technologies 
Resilience and adaptability in changing environments 
Strong collaboration and communication skills 
The confidence to challenge thinking, offer insight, and add value beyond delivery 
A practical, solutions-focused approach 

What’s in it for you 

Meaningful work within data, AI, and business advisory 
The chance to help shape client outcomes, team culture, and market presence 
A collaborative environment that values innovation, learning, and impact 
The opportunity to work with modern platforms and forward-thinking clients 

Your Next Chapter Starts Here

At Altis, we offer more than a job. We offer a career opportunity. You’ll have the tools, resources, and support to build your career, take on interesting projects, and work on challenging problems that make a real impact.

We are high performers who support each other to succeed. Our culture encourages bringing your whole self to work, embracing continuous learning, and fostering growth through diverse, complex projects. You’ll be part of a collaborative, inclusive environment where curiosity is valued, ideas are encouraged, and your contribution matters.


How We Invest in You:

A career pathway with ongoing learning and development
Exposure to varied and challenging projects across industries
A supportive, inclusive culture that values diversity and individuality
Hybrid working model
Competitive salary
A holistic health and wellness program that supports you and your family
Paid parental leave
Reward and recognition program

Let’s Start the Conversation

Apply now! We’d love to hear from you.

At Altis, Seeing Your Career Differently means creating an environment where respect, fairness, growth, and well-being are at the core of how we work. We welcome and celebrate people from all backgrounds, experiences, identities, abilities, and thinking styles, and we are committed to providing equal opportunities for all qualified applicants.

If you require adjustments or support during the application process, please let us know. We are here to ensure you can bring your best self to every stage.

Senior Data Analyst

Open to Sydney / Newcastle / Brisbane / Melbourne – Hybrid Working Model
Lead Pacific National’s enterprise Power BI transformation
Safely Every Day — That’s Our Promise

Pacific National is Australia’s largest private rail freight operator, moving millions of tonnes of essential freight across the country. We’re building an enterprise‑grade data capability — and we’re looking for a Senior Data Analyst who can elevate our reporting, modelling, and insights.

What You’ll Do

Drive high‑quality, data‑driven insights that shape operational and strategic decisions across the business.

Reporting to the Manager, Business Reporting & Analytics, you will:

Build best‑practice Power BI dashboards, reports, and semantic data models
Be a trusted analytics partner to the business, turning stakeholder needs into insights that drive decisions.
Conduct deep‑dive analytics, predictive modelling, and scenario analysis
Define KPIs/metrics and promote endorsed metric frameworks
Manage reporting and analytics work pipelines to ensure prioritised, on‑time delivery
Lift organisational data literacy through training, documentation, and self‑service enablement
Ensure data integrity, governance alignment, and analytics standards across the business

What You’ll Bring

5+ years in data analytics / BI roles
Expert Power BI skills including advanced DAX and semantic modelling (certs desirable)
Strong SQL capability and experience with data warehousing
Experience designing scalable data models and applying statistical/predictive techniques
Proven ability to manage competing priorities and deliver high‑quality outputs
Strong stakeholder engagement and communication skills
Experience delivering end‑user training and enabling self‑service BI
Exposure to Tableau, ITIL frameworks, or asset‑intensive industries is advantageous

Why join us?

At Pacific National we value the contribution of each team member. Our values underpin our commitment to create an inclusive culture where everyone is accepted.

Some of the benefits we offer are:

Paid parental leave
Novated Leasing
Fitness Passport
Purchase leave program
Access to EAP program through Sonder

We live by our Values: Make it Simple, Share More, Kindness, Gratitude, Curiosity, and Own It – helping us build strong connections and do our best work every day.

Ready to deliver what matters?

Click ‘Apply Now’ and start your journey with Pacific National. Learn more at https://www.pacificnational.com.au/careers

Pacific National acknowledges the Traditional Custodians of the land on which we work live and rail.

Financial Planning and Analysis Manager 

6 month FTC

AU Remote 

Not all heroes wear capes — but plenty of them use toilet paper 

Want to challenge yourself at a purpose-led scale up AND make a difference in the world? Come join the revolootion!

Funny name, serious business


We are a leading eco-friendly household essentials business and we donate 50% of our profits to help everyone gain access to clean water and a toilet. As of June 2024, we’ve contributed over $18 million AUD to this mission. Basically, we’re really good at making it easy to do good.

We operate scaling businesses in the UK, US, Australia, and Canada. Our amazing team of 250+ people is spread across the globe, all aiming to make the biggest possible impact for people and the planet. Over the next 5-10 years, we’re hoping to increase our annual donation tenfold. That’s a lot of toilet paper, and a lot of lives changed.

A bit about the role


You’ll be the navigator for our financial future, turning complex data into a clear map that guides our strategic decision-making. By maintaining our financial engines while our current manager is on parental leave, you’ll ensure we stay on course to hit our goals and keep making a huge impact.

This is a 6-month Fixed Term Contract, and while we are a global business, this role is Remote Australia based. You aren't just "keeping the lights on"—you'll have a primary focus on our global Supply Chain, ensuring our logistics and inventory are as efficient as they are impactful.

If you worked here this past month, here are some things you might have been involved in:

Steering the Ship: Leading the mid-year re-forecasting process and setting budgets to ensure we stay on track.
Supply Chain Deep-Dives: Quantifying the size of supply chain initiatives to make sure we’re focusing our energy where it creates the most value.
Monthly Storytelling: Crafting month-end management reporting packs that explain the "why" behind the performance.
Risk Scouting: Proactively identifying budget variances and huddling with team leads to build mitigation plans.
Finance for Non-Finance: Facilitating conversations with stakeholders across the business to elevate our collective financial literacy.
Systems Tinkering: Leveraging our operating systems (NetSuite experience is a massive bonus here!) to improve our source of truth.
Let’s talk about you


You are a strategic thinker who sees how a ripple in one area of the business affects the entire P&L. You’re a steady hand during transitions, comfortable with ambiguity, and someone who digs into discrepancies to implement permanent fixes.

What you'll bring to the huddle:

Planning Prowess: Solid FP&A with a strong experience in forecasting and budgeting.
Supply Chain Savvy: You understand the financial flow of goods from A to B.
Stakeholder Diplomacy: You are a pro at managing expectations and partnering with leaders to drive commercial outcomes.
Tech Edge: You love a good ERP; if you’ve used NetSuite, we definitely want to hear about it.
You give a crap


Do you want your work to make the world a better place? Do you want to work with other people who feel the same way? What a coincidence — us too!

Why should you work with us?


First off, as a certified B Corp™, we work to make a difference every day while maintaining the highest standards of social and environmental impact. With our most recent score of 125.5, we’re in very good company with other exceptional B Corps around the world.

We also believe that helping to make the world a better place should be rewarded accordingly – that’s why we offer competitive, market-informed salaries, meaningful support towards healthcare for our team around the world, generous paid leave, tailored learning and development opportunities, and free toilet paper (yes, you read that right).

Our engagement surveys tell us that our team is really proud (95%) to work for Who Gives A Crap and, well, we couldn’t be more proud of that.

Have you made it this far?


If you’re still reading, we think there’s a strong chance you might be our kind of person. Here’s the thing, though — research suggests that 60% of women and underrepresented folks might have already talked themselves out of applying. Even if you don’t check every box above, we want to encourage you to introduce yourself. We believe a diversity of perspectives and experiences makes a team stronger — and the stronger our team, the closer we are to delivering toilets and clean water for all.

**Applications close June 1st 2026**
